question: How much refund can I get if I withdraw from my programme at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: The refund amount depends on when your completed withdrawal form is received. If you submit the withdrawal form within the first two weeks of a new semester, you are entitled to a 50% refund of the tuition fee. If you submit the withdrawal form after the first two weeks of the new semester, no refund will be given.

question: What is the penalty for withdrawing without paying tuition fees at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: If you withdraw within the first two weeks of a semester without paying any tuition fees, you are liable to pay a penalty equivalent to 50% of the tuition fee for that semester. If you withdraw more than two weeks after the semester starts without paying any tuition fees, you are liable to pay a penalty equivalent to the full tuition fee for that semester.

question: What do I need to do before withdrawing from my programme at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: Before withdrawing, you must complete the 'Student Withdrawal Application form' available at https://eservices.xmu.edu.my/. You must also settle any outstanding fees, room rental charges, and utility bills, and return any loaned books to the library or loaned items to the responsible departments or offices.

question: What are the financial implications of applying for a study break during a semester?
answer: For foundation level students: If your study break application is received and approved before the start of a semester, you are not liable to pay any tuition fee. If approved within the first two weeks of a semester, 50% of any paid tuition fee will be treated as advanced payment for the future semester, while the other half is forfeited. If approved after the second week, all paid tuition fee for the semester is forfeited, or you may face a penalty equivalent to the full tuition fee if unpaid.

question: How does the timing of a study break application affect tuition fees?
answer: For foundation level students: Applying before the semester starts means no tuition fee is due. Applying within the first two weeks of the semester results in 50% of the paid tuition fee being treated as advanced payment, with the rest forfeited. Applying after the second week leads to forfeiture of all paid tuition fees or a penalty equal to the full tuition fee if unpaid.

question: What happens to my tuition fees if I take a study break after the semester has started?
answer: For foundation level students: If your study break is approved within the first two weeks of the semester, 50% of your paid tuition fee is treated as advanced payment for the future semester, and the other half is forfeited. If approved after the second week, all paid tuition fees are forfeited, or you may incur a penalty equal to the full tuition fee if unpaid.

question: What are the tuition fees for the Foundation in Science programme at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: For Malaysian students: The tuition fee for the Foundation in Science programme is RM19,000 per year. For international students: The tuition fee is RM20,000 per year. Additionally, international students must pay a security deposit of RM1,000 (refundable), an international student fee of RM2,500 per year, and the fees are exclusive of 6% SST.

question: What are the administrative fees for applying to a foundation programme at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: The application fee is RM100 and the registration fee is RM200 for all foundation programmes. These fees are non-refundable. For international students, additional fees include a security deposit of RM1,000 (refundable), an international student fee of RM2,500 per year, and the fees are exclusive of 6% SST.

question: What are the tuition fees for the Foundation in Arts and Social Science programme at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: For Malaysian students: The tuition fee for the Foundation in Arts and Social Science programme is RM17,000 per year. For international students: The tuition fee is RM18,000 per year. Additionally, international students must pay a security deposit of RM1,000 (refundable), an international student fee of RM2,500 per year, and the fees are exclusive of 6% SST.

question: What is the deadline to pay tuition fees for each semester?
answer: You are required to pay your tuition fee for each semester within seven (7) working days from the commencement of a new semester.

question: What happens if I do not pay my tuition fees on time?
answer: If you have defaulted on any of your tuition fee payments without informing the Finance Office about your reason for failing to make payments, you shall: (i) Be automatically barred from attending classes; (ii) Be denied access to university facilities; and (iii) Have your certificate of programme completion withheld at the time of your graduation until you have settled all your outstanding fees.

question: How will I be notified if I have outstanding fees?
answer: If you owe the university money, you will be sent reminders via email and/or SMS, and your name will appear in a list of students with outstanding fees which will be posted on notice boards.

question: How does the refund process work when I withdraw from my study programme?
answer: Any partially or fully paid fees will be refunded to you, less any deductible amount of money owing to the university, when you withdraw from your study programme. Your refund request will be processed within sixty (60) days upon receiving your completed programme withdrawal form and relevant documents (if applicable). All refund shall be free of interest and the University reserves the right to off-set the refundable amount against any outstanding fees or whatsoever payments due and owing to the University.

question: How long does it take to process a refund after withdrawing from a programme?
answer: Your refund request will be processed within sixty (60) days upon receiving your completed programme withdrawal form and relevant documents (if applicable).

question: Are there any deductions or conditions applied to the refund amount?
answer: Any partially or fully paid fees will be refunded to you, less any deductible amount of money owing to the university. The University reserves the right to off-set the refundable amount against any outstanding fees or whatsoever payments due and owing to the University.

question: What are the accepted modes of payment for fees at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: You can pay your fees in cash, with cheques, bank drafts or credit cards at the Finance Office or via direct bank-in, telegraphic transfer or online banking (ePayment).

question: Where can I pay my fees in person at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: You can pay your fees in person at the Finance Office using cash, cheques, bank drafts, or credit cards.

question: Can I pay my fees online at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: Yes, you can pay your fees via online banking (ePayment), direct bank-in, or telegraphic transfer.

question: Where can I make cash payments at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: Cash payments can be made at the Finance Office located in Room A3#711. You will receive an official receipt immediately after making the payment.

question: What happens after I make a cash payment at the Finance Office?
answer: Upon making a cash payment at the Finance Office, you will be issued an official receipt on the spot.

question: How can I pay by cheque or bank draft at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: Cheque or bank draft payments can be made at the Finance Office's cashier counter or by post. The cheque or bank draft must be issued to "XMU Jiageng Education Development Sdn Bhd" and signed and dated with the current date (post-dated cheques are not accepted). On the reverse of the cheque or bank draft, you must include your name, IC number, contact number, and purpose of payment. If you pay in person at the Finance Office (Room A3#711), you will receive an official receipt on the spot. If you send your payment by post, you must collect your official receipt from the Finance Office on your next campus visit.

question: What details are required on the back of a cheque or bank draft for payment?
answer: On the reverse of the cheque or bank draft, you must include your name, IC number, contact number, and the purpose of payment.

question: Where is the Finance Office located for cheque or bank draft payments?
answer: The Finance Office's cashier counter for cheque or bank draft payments is located in Room A3#711.

question: Where can I pay with a credit card at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: Payments with credit cards can be made at the Finance Office (Room A3#711) or through online banking.

question: Are there any processing fees for credit card payments?
answer: No processing fee will be imposed on the use of Visa, Mastercard, or UnionPay cards to make payments.

question: What is the procedure for making a credit card payment?
answer: You shall be required to key in your PIN to verify your identity before making any payments using a credit card. Once the transaction is approved by the issuing financial institution, you will be issued an official receipt by the Finance Office.

question: How can I make a payment via direct bank-in or telegraphic transfer to Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: Payments via direct bank-in or telegraphic transfer should be made to XMUM's Maybank account with the following details: Account Name - XMU Jiageng Education Development Sdn Bhd, Bank Name - Malayan Banking Berhad, Account no - 5624-3250-5328, Swift Code - MBBEMYKL, Branch - KLIA 2. After making the payment, provide a hardcopy of the bank-in or TT slip to the Finance Office or send a scanned copy to finance@xmu.edu.my. Ensure to write your name, IC/passport number, contact number, and payment purpose on the reverse side of the slip or include these details in your email. An official receipt will be issued by the Finance Office upon receipt of your payment evidence.

question: What details should I include when making a payment via bank-in or telegraphic transfer?
answer: When making a payment via bank-in or telegraphic transfer, you must include your name, IC/passport number, contact number, and the payment purpose. These details should be written on the reverse side of the bank-in or TT slip or stated in your email if you are sending a scanned copy of the slip to finance@xmu.edu.my.

question: What happens after I submit my bank-in or telegraphic transfer payment evidence?
answer: After submitting your bank-in or telegraphic transfer payment evidence (either as a hardcopy to the Finance Office or as a scanned copy to finance@xmu.edu.my), the Finance Office will issue you an official receipt once they have received and processed your payment slip.
